ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No specific SNOMED CT code identified for chicken nuggets as a prepared dish; SNOMED CT coverage for processed fast-food items is limited.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard serving of 4 chicken nuggets weighs approximately 64 g, delivering around 209 kcal, 10.6 g protein, 14.5 g fat, and 9.2 g carbohydrate, with 453 mg sodium.

nutrient_source.title: HPB Singapore Foodo — 4Pc Chicken Nuggets (D) Original (G00010017)
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: All per-100g nutrient values taken directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo canonical record G00010017 with confidence 0.95. Saturated fat, dietary fibre, trans fat, and monounsaturated/polyunsaturated fat not reported in the HPB record; these are set to -1 or estimated from USDA FoodData Central NDB 21421 (Chicken nuggets, frozen, unprepared) as a secondary cross-reference for completeness.

gi_source.title: Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC. International Tables of Glycemic Index and Glycemic Load Values: 2008. Diabetes Care 2008;31(12):2281–2283. Analogous estimate: breaded fried chicken products.
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care peer-reviewed journal
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement for this specific HPB product exists in the University of Sydney GI Database. GI of 66 is estimated from the closest analogous food category — breaded and deep-fried chicken products with wheat-flour coating — as documented in Atkinson et al. 2008 (Diabetes Care). Glycemic load per serving calculated as: (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (66 × 9.15) / 100 ≈ 6.1, using a 64 g serving size providing 9.15 g available carbohydrate.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium 708 mg per 100 g — exceeds the 600 mg/100 g upper threshold for hypertension-sensitive foods
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should avoid this product; a single 4-piece serving (64 g) delivers approximately 453 mg sodium, representing nearly 20% of the 2,300 mg daily limit, and the sodium burden is further compounded if dipping sauces are added.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Refined wheat-flour breading raises postprandial glucose; high fat content (22.6 g/100 g) and estimated trans fat (0.3 g/100 g) worsen insulin resistance
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with type 2 diabetes should limit or avoid deep-fried breaded chicken nuggets because the refined carbohydrate coating elevates postprandial blood glucose and the high saturated and trans fat content impairs insulin sensitivity; grilled or baked chicken without breading is a clinically preferable alternative.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: High total fat (22.6 g/100 g), estimated saturated fat (5.5 g/100 g), trans fat (0.3 g/100 g), and very high sodium (708 mg/100 g) collectively elevate cardiovascular risk
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Patients with cardiovascular disease should avoid regular consumption of deep-fried breaded chicken nuggets due to the combined atherogenic burden of saturated fat, trans fat, and high sodium, all of which contribute to dyslipidaemia, endothelial dysfunction, and elevated blood pressure.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Deep-frying generates advanced glycation end-products (AGEs); trans fat and saturated fat are pro-inflammatory
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Individuals with rheumatoid arthritis should avoid deep-fried breaded chicken nuggets because the high AGE load from deep-frying and the pro-inflammatory trans and saturated fat content can exacerbate systemic inflammation and joint symptoms.

llm_training.natural_language_description: 4-Piece Chicken Nuggets Original is a deep-fried, breaded chicken product sold as a fast-food portion in Singapore, catalogued in the HPB Singapore Foodo database under record G00010017. Each nugget consists of minced or formed chicken meat coated in a seasoned wheat-flour breading and deep-fried in vegetable oil, yielding a crispy exterior and a moist, tender interior. The product is classified as ultra-processed and halal-certified. Per 100 g, it delivers 326 kcal, 16.5 g protein, 22.6 g fat, and 14.3 g carbohydrate, with a notably high sodium content of 708 mg. The breaded coating contributes refined carbohydrates that elevate postprandial blood glucose, while the deep-frying process introduces saturated fat (approximately 5.5 g/100 g) and estimated trans fat (0.3 g/100 g), both of which are pro-inflammatory and atherogenic. The glycemic index is estimated at 66 (medium) based on analogous breaded fried chicken products. Clinically, this food is unsuitable for individuals managing hypertension (sodium exceeds 600 mg/100 g threshold), type 2 diabetes (refined breading and high fat impair glycaemic and insulin response), cardiovascular disease (combined saturated fat, trans fat, and sodium burden), and rheumatoid arthritis (AGE load from deep-frying). A standard serving of 4 pieces weighs approximately 64 g. Common accompaniments include ketchup, chilli sauce, or sweet-and-sour dipping sauces, which further increase sodium and sugar intake. This product is widely consumed as a snack or side dish at lunch and dinner in Singapore.
common_user_queries: How many calories in 4 chicken nuggets?
Is chicken nuggets suitable for diabetics?
Sodium content of chicken nuggets Singapore
Can I eat chicken nuggets if I have high blood pressure?

confidence_reason: Core nutrient values sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo canonical record G00010017 with a stated confidence of 0.95. Saturated fat estimated from USDA FoodData Central analogous entry (chicken nuggets, frozen) and documented. GI is an analogous estimate from a peer-reviewed source. All mandatory multilingual names are present.
notes: monounsaturated_fat_g and polyunsaturated_fat_g set to -1: detailed fatty acid breakdown not reported in the HPB Foodo record G00010017 and not available for this specific product in USDA FoodData Central Branded Foods as of 2026-04-16. water_g set to -1: moisture content not reported in the HPB source. vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg set to -1: micronutrient detail beyond the HPB-reported fields not available in the primary source for this product. saturated_fat_g (5.5 g/100 g) and dietary_fibre_g (0.5 g/100 g) are estimated from USDA FoodData Central NDB 21421 (Chicken nuggets, frozen, unprepared) as the closest approved secondary source; these values are approximate. trans_fat_g (0.3 g/100 g) is an estimate consistent with the input record's health suitability notes and USDA analogous data. GI of 66 is an analogous estimate from Atkinson et al. 2008 (Diabetes Care); no direct GI measurement for this specific HPB product exists. Serving size of 64 g is estimated based on a typical 4-piece fast-food chicken nugget portion (approximately 16 g per piece). The (D) suffix in the original name likely denotes a specific vendor or menu variant within the HPB database.
